content.text:
Objectifs de l’enseignement
This course teaches the technical and experiential design foundations needed to create immersive environments in current and future virtual, augmented, and mixed reality platforms. Starting with the original Computer Science and HCI concepts and progressing through the evolution of all supporting technologies such as visual displays for VR, AR, and MR, motion tracking, interactive 3D graphics, multimodal sensory integration, immersive audio, user interfaces, IoT, games, and experience design, the curriculum covers a wide range of literature and practice.
Contenu de la matière
Introduction to Immersive Technologies
Motion tracking, navigation and controllers
The Human behind the lenses
The present and the future of xR
Camera tracking and 3D Rendering for Immersive Environments
Modeling the Physical world
Presence, Agency and Interactivity
Sound in Immersive Environments
XR: Privacy and Security in an xR
Mode d’évaluation : Contrôle continu, examen Références
The VR Book: Human-Centered Design for Virtual Reality. Association for Computing Machinery and Morgan & Claypool Publishers
Real Virtuality: A Code of Ethical Conduct. Recommendations for Good Scientific Practice and the Consumers of VR-Technology. Frontiers in Robotics and AI 3
Tony Parisi. Learning Virtual Reality
